name: Triphala
botanicalName: Terminalia chebula, Terminalia bellirica, Phyllanthus emblica
cautions: May loosen stool
Use caution with diarrhea, dehydration, pregnancy, or medicines

name: Amalaki
cautions: May not suit some people with loose stool or cold digestion
contraindications: Use practitioner review with diabetes medicines or anticoagulants

name: Haritaki
cautions: May loosen stool
Avoid self-use in pregnancy or dehydration

name: Bibhitaki
cautions: Drying quality may aggravate dryness in some people
contraindications: Pregnancy or children without practitioner review

name: Guduchi
cautions: Use caution in autoimmune disease, liver disease, or immunosuppressant use

name: Yashtimadhu
cautions: May raise blood pressure or affect potassium
Use caution with diuretics, hypertension, kidney disease

name: Shatavari
cautions: Practitioner review needed for hormone-sensitive conditions, pregnancy, or lactation
contraindications: Pregnancy or lactation without practitioner review

name: Ashwagandha
cautions: May be sedating
Use caution with thyroid medicines, sedatives, autoimmune disease, liver disease

name: Brahmi
cautions: May cause digestive upset or drowsiness in some people

name: Mandukaparni
cautions: Use caution with liver disease or sedatives

name: Arjuna
cautions: Heart symptoms require medical review
Use caution with blood pressure and heart medicines
contraindications: Chest pain or heart disease without clinician review

name: Punarnava
cautions: Kidney, heart, liver, or diuretic medicine use needs clinician review

name: Gokshura
cautions: Use caution with kidney disease, prostate conditions, pregnancy, or medicines

name: Trikatu
botanicalName: Zingiber officinale, Piper nigrum, Piper longum
cautions: May aggravate acidity, burning, ulcers, or pitta tendency

name: Hingvastak
cautions: May aggravate heat, acidity, hypertension, or salt-restricted diets

name: Jeera
cautions: Food amounts are generally low risk; supplemental use still needs context

name: Dhaniya
cautions: Avoid if allergic to coriander or related plants

name: Saunf
cautions: Use caution with hormone-sensitive conditions or known allergy
contraindications: Pregnancy supplemental use without practitioner review

name: Ajwain
cautions: May aggravate acidity, burning, or heat tendency
contraindications: Pregnancy supplemental use without practitioner review

name: Sitopaladi
cautions: Often contains sugar; caution with diabetes or glucose control issues

name: Talisadi
cautions: May aggravate heat, acidity, or sensitive stomach

name: Chyawanprash
virya: balanced; often mildly warming due to spices
cautions: Contains sugar and many ingredients; review for diabetes, allergies, and medicine interactions

name: Dashmool
cautions: Complex group; quality and identity matter
contraindications: Serious chronic disease without clinician review

name: Kanchanar Guggulu
botanicalName: Compound formulation with Bauhinia variegata and Commiphora mukul
cautions: Requires practitioner review; possible thyroid, anticoagulant, and medicine interaction concerns

name: Punarnava Mandur
botanicalName: Compound formulation containing processed iron per classical formulary
cautions: Contains mineral ingredient; use only with qualified supervision and appropriate testing
contraindications: Liver or kidney disease without clinician review

name: Nimba
cautions: Neem oil and concentrated extracts require caution
May irritate digestion or skin in sensitive users

name: Tulasi
cautions: May interact with anticoagulant or glucose-lowering medicines
Can feel heating in high-pitta presentations

name: Pippali
cautions: Heating and pungent
Use caution with acidity, ulcers, pregnancy, and medicines

name: Maricha
cautions: Heating and sharp
May worsen burning, reflux, or mouth irritation

name: Shunthi
cautions: Can feel heating
Use caution with blood thinners and acidity

name: Haridra
cautions: May interact with blood thinners or diabetes medicines
Concentrated extracts differ from culinary use
contraindications: Pregnancy without practitioner review for concentrated use

name: Daruharidra
cautions: Berberine-containing herbs may interact with medicines
Use caution in pregnancy and liver disease

name: Musta
cautions: May be drying
Persistent diarrhea requires clinician review
contraindications: Severe dehydration or blood in stool

name: Kutki
cautions: Strong bitter herb requiring supervision
May loosen stool or interact with medicines

name: Bhumyamalaki
cautions: May interact with diabetes or blood pressure medicines
Chronic liver or kidney disease requires medical review

name: Bhringaraja
cautions: Internal use needs review in liver disease or medicines
Quality and identity matter

name: Jatamansi
cautions: May add to sedative effects
Use only with clinician review if taking psychiatric or sedative medicines

name: Vacha
cautions: Safety concerns vary by chemotype and regulation
Not for self-directed use

name: Shankhapushpi
botanicalName: Convolvulus pluricaulis / Clitoria ternatea traditions vary
cautions: May add to sedative effects
Botanical identity can vary

name: Vidanga
cautions: Sharp herb; not for pregnancy or self-directed use in children
Use caution with acidity

name: Varuna
cautions: Urinary symptoms can require medical assessment
Use caution with kidney disease

name: Pashanabheda
cautions: Kidney stones, pain, fever, or blood in urine require medical care
Do not use as emergency support

name: Manjistha
cautions: May interact with blood thinners
Use caution with pregnancy and medicines

name: Sariva
cautions: May be too cooling or heavy for some kapha patterns
Use caution with chronic disease medicines

name: Khadira
cautions: Drying and astringent
Use caution with constipation or severe dryness

name: Lodhra
cautions: Gynecologic symptoms require qualified review
Not for self-directed pregnancy use

name: Bala
cautions: Some species/constituents raise stimulant and regulatory concerns
Use caution with hypertension and medicines
contraindications: Sedative or stimulant medication without clinician review

name: Atibala
cautions: Use caution in pregnancy, chronic disease, and polypharmacy
Quality identification matters

name: Rasna
cautions: Joint pain with swelling, fever, trauma, or chronic disease needs clinician review

name: Nirgundi
cautions: Internal use requires guidance
May be heating or drying

name: Eranda
cautions: Castor oil is strongly laxative
Seeds are toxic and not for use

name: Shigru
cautions: Heating and sharp; use caution with thyroid medicines and pregnancy

name: Lasuna
cautions: May interact with blood thinners and surgery planning
May aggravate reflux or heat

name: Hingu
cautions: Heating and pungent
Use caution in pregnancy and reflux

name: Lavanga
cautions: Essential oil can irritate skin and mucosa
Heating in pitta patterns
contraindications: Pregnancy without practitioner review for concentrated use

name: Ela
cautions: Usually low risk as culinary spice
Concentrated use needs review with gallbladder disease
contraindications: Gallbladder disease without clinician review for concentrated use

name: Tvak
cautions: Heating spice; cassia cinnamon has coumarin safety concerns in high intake
May interact with diabetes medicines
contraindications: Liver disease without clinician review for high intake

name: Tejapatra
cautions: Usually low risk as culinary spice
Concentrated use needs review in pregnancy
contraindications: Pregnancy without practitioner review for concentrated use

name: Methi
traditionalUses: lactation folk use only under guidance
cautions: May lower blood glucose
May interact with anticoagulants and diabetes medicines

name: Kumari
cautions: Aloe latex is laxative and not the same as gel
Internal use requires review

name: Bakuchi
cautions: Photosensitizing and potentially irritating
Requires qualified supervision

name: Chitraka
cautions: Very heating and sharp
Not for self-directed use

name: Devadaru
cautions: Heating and aromatic
Use caution with pregnancy and chronic disease

name: Chandana
cautions: Internal use is not self-care
Sustainability and adulteration concerns
contraindications: Kidney disease without clinician review for internal use

name: Ushira
cautions: May be too cooling for cold-dominant patterns
Use caution with chronic kidney disease

name: Apamarga
traditionalUses: digestive and urinary contexts under guidance
cautions: Sharp and heating
Not for pregnancy or self-directed child use
